title: Venezuelan Lechon Asado
description: A traditional Venezuelan dish featuring a slow-roasted pork shoulder marinated in a mixture of citrus, spices, and herbs.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together lime juice, olive oil, garlic, oregano, coriander, cumin, salt, and black pepper.
Add the pork shoulder to the marinade and mix well.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Remove the pork from the marinade and place it in a large Dutch oven or oven-safe pot.
Cover the pot with a lid and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Roast the pork for 2 hours and 30 minutes.
Remove the pot from the oven and let the pork rest for 10 minutes.
Strain the juices from the pot and discard the solids.
Serve the pork with the strained juices spooned over the top.
Garnish with chopped cilantro and serve with rice, beans, or boiled potatoes.
